title: Trade Repository (TR) Data Collections
summary: The Bank of England collects trade repository data under UK EMIR (derivatives) and UK SFTR (securities financing transactions) to support financial stability objectives and market transparency following post-2008 crisis reforms. Data is used by multiple Bank committees (FPC, PRC, MPC, FMIC) and feeds into the Financial Stability Report. Final rules for updated reporting requirements come into force on 26 January 2026.
key_points: Data collected covers five derivatives asset classes: interest rates, foreign exchange, commodities, credit default swaps, and equities
Trade repositories aggregate transaction-level data from reporting firms and submit to financial authorities
Bank and FCA share UK EMIR reporting responsibilities; Bank handles CCPs, FCA handles other counterparties
New UK EMIR reporting requirements finalised August 2025, effective 26 January 2026
September 2024 regime aligned UK derivatives reporting with CPMI-IOSCO harmonisation guidance
Data informs FPC, PRC, MPC, and FMIC committees plus Financial Stability Reports and research
why_it_matters: This source describes the regulatory infrastructure for derivatives and SFT transparency rather than providing macro catalysts or market-moving information. It is relevant for understanding post-crisis market oversight but does not itself constitute a market event or catalyst.
market_structure_context: Trade repository data supports financial stability oversight by providing authorities visibility into derivatives and securities financing markets. The September 2024 updates aligned UK reporting with CPMI-IOSCO harmonisation standards for globally consistent OTC derivatives data.
